behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (HTTP header/body parsing + HMAC verification + enqueue suppression), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process HTTP harness is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text does not require real external infrastructure, only correct handler behavior and non-forwarding.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with an in-process HTTP test server and real middleware wiring. Send a body with an invalid X-Signature-256 and assert 401, and assert queue write method/store received zero writes.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is ordering across modules (request handler + persistence adapter + response path). Needs real queue implementation wiring, not unit stubs. REAL-INFRA: no explicit requirement for real network/subprocess; local persistence is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text does not require external API/OS effects beyond app-local persistent queue semantics.
behaviors.test_description: With real queue adapter (e.g., local DB/file-backed queue) in test env, instrument enqueue completion and response send time. Assert enqueue commit timestamp occurs before 200 response is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this verifies async decoupling between enqueue/ack path and worker processing path across modules. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process slow worker stub; no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text only requires non-blocking orchestration, not real external infra contracts.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + worker in-process; make worker intentionally sleep. Send request and assert response is returned before worker completion signal, while event still eventually processes.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: timing/concurrency SLO is externally visible and sensitive to runtime scheduling and real HTTP serving behavior; in-process mocks can hide queue/backpressure/event-loop contention. SYSTEM TRIAD trigger: externally visible response-time contract under concurrent load. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Start real service process, generate concurrent traffic including slow prior events, and measure real client-observed latency for new webhook requests. Assert p95 (or strict per-request bound per spec) is <= 3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: duplicate suppression depends on interaction between handler, idempotency key logic, and persistent queue/storage state. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local persistence, no real external network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text does not require external infra contract, only correctness of dedupe with persisted state.
behaviors.test_description: Using real local persistence queue schema/adapter, send same event ID twice via handler. Assert only one persisted queue record exists and second request follows expected duplicate path.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: SYSTEM TRIAD trigger: explicit external error-path HTTP contract (status + header) that clients rely on. MOCK-HIDE: faked transport can miss real header/status behavior through actual server stack. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Run service over real HTTP, force queue-full condition, send webhook request, and assert client receives 503 and correct Retry-After header formatting/value.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is queue implementation + persistence behavior across modules and config, not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue is sufficient; no required real external service/subprocess stated. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text does not require remote API/OS-specific behavior beyond local persistent enforcement.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate queue with real persistence backend and small configured capacity; enqueue until full, then enqueue one more and assert deterministic rejection/error from the real queue implementation.
